assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: The response clearly recommends using Regular release channel with maintenance exclusions, showing YAML configuration with maintenanceExclusions for NO_UPGRADES scope

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: Response shows NO_UPGRADES exclusions but doesn't specifically mention 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusions or the --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support option for persistent exclusions

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: Response provides detailed maintenance window configuration with Saturday maintenance windows (FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=SA) and specific time slots (02:00-06:00)

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention cluster disruption budget or disruption intervals with their default/maximum values

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: Response recommends Regular release channel instead of Stable or Extended channels for production clusters in financial services environments

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: Response does not explicitly state that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended or mention monthly patches as a minimum requirement

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: Response shows staged environments (dev/staging/prod) but doesn't mention rollout sequencing or cluster disruption budget for controlling control plane patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response provides multiple gcloud commands including cluster update commands for maintenance exclusions and upgrade commands, though focused more on monitoring and emergency procedures
